results.title: kagent | Bringing Agentic AI to cloud native
excerpts:
Section Title: Bringing Agentic AI to cloud native
Content:
Open-source tools for DevOps and platform engineers to build, deploy, and run AI-powered solutions in Kubernetes. From intelligent agents to MCP servers.
Get Started [View on GitHub](https://github.com/kagent-dev)
Section Title: Bringing Agentic AI to cloud native > Our projects > kagent
Content:
Agentic AI Framework
An open-source framework for running AI agents in Kubernetes, automating complex DevOps operations and troubleshooting tasks with intelligent workflows.
Quick Start [GitHub](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent)
Section Title: Bringing Agentic AI to cloud native > Our projects > kmcp
Content:
MCP Kubernetes toolkit
The easiest way to create, deploy, and securely run MCP servers on Kubernetes — from development to production scale.
Quick Start [GitHub](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kmcp)

**kagent** is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents. Kubernetes is the most popular orchestration platform for running workloads, and **kagent** makes it easy to build, deploy and manage AI agents in Kubernetes. The **kagent** framework is designed to be easy to understand and use, and to provide a flexible and powerful way to build and manage AI agents.
 ... 
Section Title: kagent-dev/kagent > Technical Details > Core Concepts
Content:
**Agents** : Agents are the main building block of kagent. They are a system prompt, a set of tools and agents, and an LLM configuration represented with a Kubernetes custom resource called "Agent". **LLM Providers** : Kagent supports multiple LLM providers, including [OpenAI](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/openai) , [Azure OpenAI](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/azure-openai) , [Anthropic](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/anthropic) , [Google Vertex AI](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/google-vertexai) , [Ollama](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/ollama) and any other [custom providers and models](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/supported-providers/custom-models) accessible via AI gateways. Providers are represented by the ModelConfig resource. **MCP Tools** : Agents can connect to any MCP server that provides tools.
 ... 
Section Title: kagent-dev/kagent > Technical Details > Core Principles
Content:
**Kubernetes Native** : Kagent is designed to be easy to understand and use, and to provide a flexible and powerful way to build and manage AI agents.
**Extensible** : Kagent is designed to be extensible, so you can add your own agents and tools.
**Flexible** : Kagent is designed to be flexible, to suit any AI agent use case.
**Observable** : Kagent is designed to be observable, so you can monitor the agents and tools using all common monitoring frameworks.
**Declarative** : Kagent is designed to be declarative, so you can define the agents and tools in a YAML file.
**Testable** : Kagent is designed to be tested and debugged easily. This is especially important for AI agent applications.
Section Title: kagent-dev/kagent > Technical Details > Architecture
Content:
The kagent framework is designed to be easy to understand and use, and to provide a flexible and powerful way to build and manage AI agents.
Kagent has 4 core components:
**Controller** : The controller is a Kubernetes controller that watches the kagent custom resources and creates the necessary resources to run the agents.
**UI** : The UI is a web UI that allows you to manage the agents and tools.
**Engine** : The engine runs your agents using [ADK](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/) .
**CLI** : The CLI is a command-line tool that allows you to manage the agents and tools.
 ...

Content:

kagent is a [Cloud Native Computing Foundation](https://cncf.io) project.

results.title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes - The New Stack
excerpts:
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes
Content:
Solo.io introduces a new project designed to help users build and run AI agents to speed up Kubernetes workflows.
Mar 17th, 2025 6:00am by [Heather Joslyn](https://thenewstack.io/author/hjoslyn/ "Posts by Heather Joslyn")
[Solo.io](https://solo.io?utm_content=inline+mention) , a cloud native application networking company, today announced kagent, a new open source framework designed to help users build and run [AI agents](https://thenewstack.io/ai-agents/) to speed up Kubernetes workflows.
[Kagent](https://kagent.dev/) , aimed at [DevOps](https://thenewstack.io/devops/) and [platform engineers](https://thenewstack.io/platform-engineering/) , offers tools, resources and AI agents that can help automate tasks such as configuration, troubleshooting, observability and network security.
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes
Content:
The framework integrates with other cloud native tools through an architecture built on the [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://thenewstack.io/mcp-the-missing-link-between-ai-agents-and-apis/) . MCP, introduced by Anthropic in November, is intended to standardize how AI models integrate with APIs.
Kagent, built on [Microsoft’s](https://aka.ms/modelmondays?utm_content=inline+mention) open source framework [AutoGen](https://thenewstack.io/a-developers-guide-to-the-autogen-ai-agent-framework/) , holds an [Apache 2.0](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) open source license.
The project began as an internal solution to a customer problem, according to [Lin Sun](https://thenewstack.io/author/lin-sun/) , Solo.io’s senior director of open source.
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes
Content:
“We have hundreds of customers who are either running our gateway or mesh solutions,” Sun told The New Stack. “So as we were working with these customers, we have our internal supporting team. They are the face to work with these customers, and they help customers figure out what’s the right solution in the cloud native ecosystem? They help customers solve general problems and also domain-specific problems.”
In the wake of Hurricane Helene’s destructive path last fall through the U.S. Southeast, an insurance company that was a client of Solo.io’s reached out for help, after the insurer’s customers began trying to file online claims on their damaged homes.
“That weekend our team was called in for help, because there was some issue with the production side, and we were jumping to troubleshooting and try to identify where there are, like, 10 network hops. And where is the problem in that network hub?”
 ... 
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes > A Wish List for the Community
Content:
Solo.io, which builds its products on open source projects, intends to donate kagent to the [Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)](https://cncf.io/?utm_content=inline+mention) , Sun said. If this happens, it would follow the company’s donation in November of Gloo Gateway, a popular open source API gateway, to the CNCF.
The project, now called [kgateway](https://kgateway.dev/) , was named an official CNCF Sandbox project this month.
Kagent’s initial launch includes tools for Argo, Helm, Istio, and [Kubernetes](https://thenewstack.io/kubernetes/) , along with a Grafana and Prometheus [observability](https://thenewstack.io/observability/) tool. It also includes a cloud native expert knowledge base that can extend with any MCP-compatible tool server.
The framework includes three layers:
 ... 
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes > A Wish List for the Community
Content:
“What we are hoping for is this kagent as an inspiration for the community,” Sun said. “We seeded the project with a few sample agents, a few tools and also a framework integrated with Kubernetes. And then we’re hoping the rest of the community can help us enhance what we build, and also help us [add additional agents](https://kagent.dev/tools) to the catalog to greatly benefit the rest of the ecosystem.
“What I’m  envisioning is, for every critical CNCF projects or cloud native projects out there, we have an agent in the catalog, so that when a new user comes to the cloud native landscape, they can have a project-specific agent sitting next to them, and they could even call multiple agents.”
Sun has an extensive wish list. In addition to urging users to try the existing tools and agents and contribute improvements, she offers other ideas.
 ... 
Section Title: Meet Kagent, Open Source Framework for AI Agents in Kubernetes > A Wish List for the Community
Content:
[Heather Joslyn is the former editor-in-chief of The New Stack. She previously worked as editor-in-chief of Container Solutions, a Cloud Native consulting company, and as an editor/reporter at The Chronicle of Philanthropy and the Baltimore City Paper. Read more from Heather Joslyn](https://thenewstack.io/author/hjoslyn/)
SHARE THIS STORY

results.title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment | by Purushotham Reddy | Medium
excerpts:
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment
Content:
Purushotham Reddy
11 min read
·
Oct 1, 2025
[](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F_%2Fvote%2Fp%2Faf08af1ed583&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fpurushothamkdr453.medium.com%2Fkagent-agentic-ai-framework-for-cloud-native-environment-af08af1ed583&user=Purushotham+Reddy&userId=66a97950346c&source=---header_actions--af08af1ed583---------------------clap_footer------------------)
--
[](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2F_%2Fbookmark%2Fp%2Faf08af1ed583&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fpurushothamkdr453.medium.com%2Fkagent-agentic-ai-framework-for-cloud-native-environment-af08af1ed583&source=---header_actions--af08af1ed583---------------------bookmark_footer------------------)
Listen
Share
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:**
Content:
kagent is an agentic AI framework for cloud native environment. under this framwork we have 2 projects [kagent](https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/getting-started/quickstart) & [kmcp](https://kagent.dev/docs/kmcp/introduction) .
kagent Agentic AI framework is an opensource which helps in deploying & running AI powered solutions on Kubernetes. It helps devops & SRE engineers in fixing issues automatically with intelligent decision making capabilities by leveraging AI, finding connection issues in multi-hop communication etc etc..
On the other hand [kmcp](https://kagent.dev/docs/kmcp/introduction) project is developing, deploying & running MCP servers on kuberntes environment.
In this blog we are going to talk about Kagent. In future blogs we can discuss about kmcp.
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:** > **Architecture:**
Content:
Kagent archiecture comprises following components.
Controller
AppEngine
CLI
Dashboard
Agents
Tools
**Kagent Controller** is a kubernetes controller. Its looks for CRDS and applies those crd confiurations. We will talk more about these CRDs later.
**AppEngine:** This is the core component of kagent. It is built on top of agent development kit. it helps in adding our own tools & agents.
**Agents:** Agents are the components through which the user interacts. User shoots query to Agent in natural langague. Agent leverages LLM and responds to user queries. We can create our own agents.
Agent contain 2 important characteristics i.e System Prompt & Tools.
**SystemPrompt:** Set of instructions that define behavior & capabilities.
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:** > **Architecture:**
Content:
**Tools:** Tools are the functions that helps agent to connect to specific environment. For example if agent has to get some details about k8s cluster it needs tools like list_pods, patch_resource etc etc. So tool is nothing but a function that helps agents to connect to environment.
**Cli:** There is a cli named kagent which helps in connecting to kagent environment.
**Dashboard:** Besides having cli, we also have another option i.e dashboard through which we can connect & interact with Agents.
Besides these, Kagent supports MCP(model context Protocol) & A2A protocol(Agent to Agent).
by now we got a brief idea, what kagent is & use of it. Lets try to install or setup it on k8s cluster. You can Use Any Kubernetes cluster. I am going to RKE2 k8s cluster which will be setup on vagrant machin on my laptop.
 ... 
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:** > **Setup Details:**
Content:
```
# -*- mode: ruby -*-  
# vi: set ft=ruby :  
  
ENV['VAGRANT_NO_PARALLEL'] = 'yes'  
  
VAGRANT_BOX         = "generic/ubuntu2204"  
#VAGRANT_BOX_VERSION = "4.2.10"  
CPUS_MASTER_NODE    = 6   
MEMORY_MASTER_NODE  = 10240   
MASTER_NODES_COUNT  = 1  
  
  
  
Vagrant.configure(2) do |config|  
  
# Kubernetes Master Servers  
(1..MASTER_NODES_COUNT).each do |i|  
  
  config.vm.define "kmaster#{i}" do |node|  
  
    node.vm.box               = VAGRANT_BOX  
    node.vm.box_check_update  = false  
    #node.vm.box_version       = VAGRANT_BOX_VERSION  
    node.vm.hostname          = "kmaster#{i}.example.com"  
    node.vm.disk :disk, size: "50GB", primary: true  
    node.vm.disk :disk, size: "30GB", name: "extra_storage"  
    node.vm.disk :disk, size: "30GB", name: "extra_storage1"  
    node.vm.disk :disk, size: "30GB", name: "extra_storage2"  
    #node.vm.network
 ... 
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:** > **Setup Details:**
Content:
```
providers:  
  default: gemini  
  gemini:  
    apiKeySecretKey: GOOGLE_API_KEY  
    apiKeySecretRef: kagent-gemini  
    model: gemini-2.5-flash  
    provider: Gemini  
ui:  
  service:  
    type: LoadBalancer
```
Install kagent helm chart using above values.
```
helm install kagent oci://ghcr.io/kagent-dev/kagent/helm/kagent --namespace kagent -f kagent-values.yaml
```
now you should see lot of pods coming up.
```
kubectl get pods -n kagent
```
Press enter or click to view image in full size
if you see the above screenshot it contains kagent controller pod, kagent ui pod and other agent pods. We talked about controller and ui components under architecture. There are many agents pods also deployed. kagent ships with many agents like istio agent, k8s agnent, observability agent etc etc. If you want you can disable these. For now lets go with default installation.
 ... 
Section Title: Kagent: Agentic AI framework for cloud native environment > **Introduction:** > **Setup Details:**
Content:
In this blog we have used dashboard or ui. Instead of using dashboard we can use cli as well. kagent cli binary or utility can be used to perform all the above actions i.e installing kagent, listing current agents, invoking agent with query etc…
In this blog we have just scratched the surface i.e just used default shipped agents. We can create our own agents, new model configs, integrating kagent with mcp servers, A2A protocol etc etc..
We can discuss more about these features in upcoming blogs.
I hope this blog is infromative to you. please share your feedback if you have any.

results.title: Building Agents On Kubernetes With Kagent
excerpts:
Section Title: Building Agents On Kubernetes With Kagent
Content:
One of the most popular advancements in cloud-native at this time is LLMs and AI Agents, which can help you troubleshoot your environment, build out new environments, and even template out a code base for you.
The problem is that there hasn't been a way to truly run Agents in a practical manner in Kubernetes, which is currently the most popular way organizations are orchestrating distributed systems.
In this blog post, you'll learn how to implement and build Agents in k8s with kagent.

results.title: KAgent: Open-Source Agentic AI Framework for Autonomous Systems | by Payal Godhani | Medium
excerpts:
Section Title: How to Get Started with KAgent > Step 5: Deploy Your Agent
Content:
Once you’ve developed your agent, you can deploy it in your environment. KAgent supports integration with external systems and APIs, so you can run the agent in the cloud, on a server, or even locally.

results.title: What are AI agents?
excerpts:
Blog Docs Tools Agents [GitHub](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent) Community Enterprise Search Toggle theme Get Started
Toggle theme
Documentation
kagent
kmcp
 ... 
Section Title: Agents # > Agents as Tools # > MCP server endpoint #
Content:
A2A-enabled agents are automatically exposed as an MCP server on the kagent controller. The MCP endpoint is available at `/mcp` on the same port as the A2A endpoint (default 8083).
For more information, see the MCP tools guide.
Kagent Lab: Discover kagent and kmcp
Free, on‑demand lab: build custom AI agents with kagent and integrate tools via kmcp on Kubernetes.
[Start the Lab](https://www.solo.io/resources/lab/kagent-lab-discover-kagent-kmcp/?utm_source=kagent-website&utm_medium=referral)

was created at [Solo.io](https://solo.io/) and is a [Cloud Native Computing Foundation](https://cncf.io/) sandbox project
